justification: All pass criteria met: (1) SLA math clearly identified with specific numbers — 99.9% Onfido makes 99.95% team SLA unachievable, realistic ceiling ~99.85%. (2) Multiple mitigation options recommended — provisional/async registration, cached approval, manual review queue, plus formal SLA revision. (3) Circuit breaker gap explicitly called out as 'single biggest operational risk' with cascade failure analysis. (4) Health endpoint / Onfido coupling issue identified with specific recommendation to separate liveness from dependency checks. (5) Verdict is NOT READY with escalation to MJ (architecture) and Bird (business rules) for SLA decision.

observations.text: SLA math is precise and well-presented with specific downtime numbers (26.3 min vs 43.8 min/month) and realistic ceiling estimate of 99.85%
Three distinct fallback strategies offered: provisional registration, cached approval, manual review queue
Circuit breaker gap thoroughly analyzed with cascade failure scenario (thread pool exhaustion, impact on non-KYC paths)
Health check coupling identified with actionable fix: move Onfido to readiness probe, not liveness
Escalation routing is specific and appropriate — architecture decision to MJ, business rules to Bird, implementation fix to Shaq
Additional failure modes caught beyond rubric requirements: rate limiting (429), unexpected payloads, missing retry with backoff
Prioritized recommendations split into must-haves before production, immediately after launch, and nice-to-haves — actionable and well-ordered
